issues.title: Create isolated v2 packages, runtime server seam, and TUI/GUI client bases
issues.what_to_build: Create `packages/lazydiff-v2-{protocol,diff,core}` plus `apps/lazydiff-v2-{server,tui,gui}` isolated from legacy `src/app.rs`. The server owns AppCore/runtime state and serves semantic frames. TUI and GUI apps are thin clients/renderers that consume the same frame data and do not own product behavior directly.

acceptance_criteria.text: `packages/lazydiff-v2-diff` exists and exposes a small parsed-diff interface.
`packages/lazydiff-v2-protocol` exists and defines renderer-agnostic semantic frames.
`packages/lazydiff-v2-core` exists and owns the initial AppCore/contribution registry.
`apps/lazydiff-v2-server` exists and serves a patch-backed frame without importing legacy `src/app.rs` modules.
`apps/lazydiff-v2-tui` exists and renders server frames without importing legacy `src/app.rs` modules.
`apps/lazydiff-v2-gui` exists and renders server frames without importing legacy `src/app.rs` modules.
The first protocol boundary is explicit: TUI and GUI client code translate server frame data into host output and do not own diff navigation/product state.
`cargo run -p lazydiff-v2-server -- patch <file>` serves a semantic frame.
`cargo run -p lazydiff-v2-tui -- --server <host:port>` renders file names and diff lines from the server.
`cargo run -p lazydiff-v2-gui -- --server <host:port>` consumes the same server frame.
Focused unit tests cover the v2 diff parser smoke path.

issues.verification: cargo test -p lazydiff-v2-protocol -p lazydiff-v2-diff -p lazydiff-v2-core -p lazydiff-v2-server -p lazydiff-v2-tui && cargo check -p lazydiff-v2-gui && cargo run -p lazydiff-v2-server -- --help && cargo run -p lazydiff-v2-tui -- --help && cargo run -p lazydiff-v2-gui -- --help
issues.north_star_check: Did this create an isolated server-owned v2 seam without adding adapter glue to the legacy app or baking product behavior into the TUI/GUI renderers?

issues.title: Add termwright smoke test for v2 diff rendering
issues.what_to_build: Add `scripts/test-v2-diff-render-termwright.sh` and include it in `scripts/tui-verify.sh` discovery by naming convention. It should stage a synthetic diff, run `lazydiff-v2`, and assert file name plus added/removed/context lines render.

acceptance_criteria.text: Termwright v2 diff render smoke test exists and is executable.
The test asserts file name and changed lines are visible.

issues.north_star_check: Does v2 get Mode-B coverage from the first TUI-visible slice?
